data.title: Portland General Electric Bets Big on Washington Deal
data.description:
Portland General Electric ((POR)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Portland General Electric’s latest earnings call struck an upbeat tone, with management leaning into a transformative Washington acquisition, accelerating industrial and data center demand, and a deep clean‑energy pipeline. While they acknowledged weather‑driven earnings pressure, regulatory risk, and heavy near‑term capital needs, the company’s confidence in sustaining 5%–7% EPS and dividend growth clearly dominated the discussion.

Strategic Washington Acquisition

PGE unveiled a definitive $1.9 billion deal to acquire PacifiCorp’s Washington electric utility business, which would expand its overall portfolio by about 18% and add roughly 140,000 customers across 2,700 square miles. Manulife’s John Hancock unit will take a 49% minority stake, and management expects the transaction to be accretive in its first full year.

Strong 2025 Financial Results (GAAP and Non-GAAP)

For 2025, PGE reported GAAP net income of $306 million, or $2.77 per diluted share, and non‑GAAP net income of $336 million, or $3.05 per share, after stripping out transformation and optimization expenses. Despite weather headwinds, management framed these results as evidence that the core business is performing ahead of headline GAAP numbers.

Affirmed and Enhanced Long-Term Growth Targets

The company reaffirmed its long‑term EPS and dividend growth target of 5%–7% annually and made clear that the Washington acquisition should place it above the midpoint of that range. That stance signals confidence that the combined platform, supported by new customers and assets, can sustain faster growth without fundamentally altering its risk profile.

2026 Guidance and Load Growth Expectations

For 2026, PGE guided to EPS of $3.33–$3.53 per share, underpinned by robust demand trends. Management also projected weather‑adjusted load growth of 2.5%–3.5% in 2026 and roughly 3% annually through 2030, underscoring a structurally stronger demand backdrop than is typical for a regulated utility.

Robust Industrial and Data Center Demand

Industrial load climbed 14% year over year, helping lift total load by 3.8%, or 4.7% on a weather‑adjusted basis, a standout performance in the sector. The company signed five new data center contracts totaling 430 MW for late 2025 and early 2026 and highlighted a pipeline of about 1.7 GW of large‑load requests, pointing to durable demand from digital infrastructure.

RFP and Clean Energy Pipeline

PGE’s 2025 request for proposals produced a shortlist of around 5 GW of renewable and non‑emitting capacity, with final selections expected near 2.5 GW through a mix of build‑transfer deals and PPAs. The company also announced the Biglow and Wheatridge expansions, combining solar and batteries with in‑service dates by 2027 and access to 30%–40% federal tax credits to help blunt customer bill impacts.

Cost Management and O&M Efficiencies

The utility exceeded its 2025 cost‑management goals, cutting its overall cost structure by about $25 million net of transformation expenses. Management emphasized that these operational and O&M efficiencies should grow over time and become durable, giving PGE more flexibility to fund capital projects while keeping rates competitive.

Strong Liquidity, Credit Profile and Financing Plan

PGE ended the year with $954 million of total liquidity and CFO‑to‑debt above 19%, helping Moody’s move its outlook from negative to stable. To fund the Washington deal, it secured bridge financing from major banks plus Manulife’s equity, and laid out a permanent plan including $600 million of minority equity, $700 million of secured debt at the Washington utility, $600 million at a holdco, an upsized $500 million ATM, and up to $350 million of 2026 debt issuance.

Regulatory and Operational Wins

On the regulatory front, PGE gained approval for the Seaside battery project and reached a constructive stipulation on its distributed system plan, both key to integrating more renewables. It is also advancing a POWER Act‑aligned tariff in Oregon that would create a separate data center customer class and support affordability for residential customers, with completion targeted in the second quarter.

Wildfire Risk Management and Operational Integration

Executives stressed the company’s mature, year‑round wildfire mitigation programs as a core competency it will extend to Washington operations. They noted that high‑fire‑risk exposure in Washington is relatively low, at about 2% of the service territory and 20 distribution miles, broadly mirroring Oregon’s risk profile and easing investor concerns about wildfire‑related liability.

Weather-Related Earnings Headwind

The company flagged unusually warm weather in November and December 2025 as a major earnings drag, cutting full‑year EPS by $0.17, with December alone accounting for around $0.14. That anomaly weighed on reported GAAP results but was framed as a transitory headwind rather than a sign of weakening underlying demand.

Regulatory and Approval Risk

Management acknowledged that the Washington acquisition faces layered approvals in Oregon, Washington, and at the federal level, with review periods that could stretch 11–12 months or longer. The differing standards, from Oregon’s “no harm” test to Washington’s “net benefit” requirement, introduce timing and outcome risk that could affect when and how value from the transaction is realized.

Integration and Financing Uncertainties

PGE also pointed to uncertainties around final capital structure and holdco approval, which will drive the mix of debt and equity it ultimately issues. The company currently expects about $300 million of base equity needs in 2026, tapering to roughly $50 million in 2027, plus around $350 million of equity tied to 2023 RFP projects, creating some potential dilution depending on market conditions.

Potential Break Fees and Transaction Costs

The Washington deal includes symmetrical break‑fee provisions and related contingencies that could cost approximately $35 million if triggered. While management views these as standard protections, they are a non‑trivial risk factor investors must weigh in assessing the probability and economics of deal closure.

Residential Load Softness

Residential load slipped 1.8% year over year, though it rose 0.4% on a weather‑adjusted basis, even as residential customer counts increased 1.3%. The company framed this as a manageable headwind that is more than offset by booming industrial and data center demand, but it remains a trend to monitor for rate design and revenue stability.

Data Center Tariff Impacts and Stakeholder Sensitivity

PGE’s proposed data center tariff would raise prices for those customers by about 25% while initially lowering residential and small business rates by roughly 2%, shifting cost responsibility toward large users. Management expects some pushback from data center operators and regulators and signaled that negotiations will be key to balancing growth, fairness, and affordability.

Concentration of CapEx and Timing Risk

A heavy near‑term capital slate, including RFP awards, Biglow, Wheatridge, and additional storage procurements, will test the company’s execution and financing capacity. With around 2.5 GW of projects expected to be selected and most targeted to be in service by 2027, any permitting, supply chain, or construction delays could pressure both earnings and cash flows.

GAAP vs. Non-GAAP Adjustment Reliance

The 2025 non‑GAAP EPS of $3.05 stands well above the GAAP figure of $2.77, driven by adjustments for transformation and optimization costs that management characterizes as one‑time in nature. That gap underscores PGE’s reliance on adjusted metrics to tell its performance story and is an area where investors will watch closely for genuine normalization versus recurring charges.

Forward-Looking Guidance and Outlook

Looking ahead, PGE reaffirmed 2026 EPS guidance of $3.33–$3.53 per share and projected 2026 weather‑adjusted load growth of 2.5%–3.5%, supporting its roughly 3% annual load growth view through 2030. Coupled with a solid liquidity position, planned equity and debt issuance, and the expected year‑one accretion from the Washington deal, management argued that its 5%–7% EPS and dividend growth trajectory remains intact.

PGE’s earnings call painted the picture of a utility leaning into growth, anchored by a large Washington acquisition, strong industrial demand, and a sizeable clean‑energy build‑out. Execution, regulatory approvals, and weather remain key swing factors, but the company’s strengthened balance sheet, cost discipline, and reaffirmed growth targets leave investors with a generally constructive story for the years ahead.

data.title: B2Gold Posts Record 2025 Revenue and Solid Margins as Goose Mine Ramps Up
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

B2Gold ( (TSE:BTO) ) just unveiled an announcement.

B2Gold reported strong operational and financial performance for 2025, with consolidated gold production of 979,604 ounces, driven by better-than-expected output at its Fekola, Masbate and Otjikoto mines and the ramp-up of the Goose Mine, which reached commercial production in October. The miner delivered annual cash operating costs of $769 per ounce and all-in sustaining costs of $1,584 per ounce, both at or below guidance, underpinned by higher production and lower fuel costs.

The company posted a record $3.06 billion in revenue on sales of 927,797 ounces at an average realized gold price of $3,299 per ounce, translating into 2025 net income attributable to shareholders of $402 million, or $0.30 per share, and adjusted net income of $612 million, or $0.46 per share. With operating cash flow before working capital of $940 million, a solid cash balance of $380 million at year-end, and 2026 production guidance of 820,000 to 970,000 ounces, B2Gold reinforced its financial resilience and capital-return plans by declaring a Q1 2026 dividend of US$0.02 per share.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:BTO) stock is a Hold with a C$6.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on B2Gold stock, see the TSE:BTO Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSE:BTO is a Neutral.

B2Gold’s overall stock score reflects a combination of strong operational achievements and financial challenges. The positive earnings call and technical indicators provide a favorable outlook, but high valuation and profitability issues weigh on the score. The company needs to address cash flow and profitability to enhance its financial position.

The company operates the Fekola, Masbate and Otjikoto mines and recently brought the Goose Mine into commercial production, focusing on large-scale gold production and disciplined cost management in key mining jurisdictions.

data.title: Lundin Gold Expands High-Grade Footprint at Fruta del Norte With Record Drilling Campaign
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Lundin Gold ( (TSE:LUG) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Lundin Gold reported further high-grade drilling results from its Fruta del Norte South and FDN East programs in Ecuador, including intercepts such as 91.32 grams per tonne of gold over 20.65 metres, which confirm and expand the mineralized footprint. The company has enlarged the known FDN East trend from 500 to 650 metres and continues to identify new mineralized veins north and south of existing resources, supporting the potential for additional mineral reserves and extended mine life.

The miner completed over 121,000 metres of drilling in 2025 and plans at least 133,000 metres in 2026 with 17 rigs, marking its largest campaign on the Fruta del Norte land package. These results and the expanded drilling program reinforce Fruta del Norte’s status as a large, high-grade gold system and point to a robust growth pipeline, with implications for future production, reserve upgrades, and long-term value creation for stakeholders.

More about Lundin Gold

Lundin Gold Inc. is a Canada-based precious metals producer focused on the Fruta del Norte underground gold mine in southeast Ecuador. The company operates in the gold mining industry, targeting high-grade epithermal gold deposits and pursuing near-mine exploration to extend mine life and expand mineral reserves around its flagship asset.

Find detailed analytics on LUG stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: BriaPro to Acquire BriaCell’s Soluble CD80 License in Strategic Cancer Immunotherapy Deal
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

BriaCell Therapeutics ( (TSE:BCT) ) has shared an update.

BriaCell Therapeutics and its majority-owned subsidiary BriaPro Therapeutics have signed a definitive asset purchase agreement under which BriaPro will acquire BriaCell’s exclusive license to develop and commercialize the Soluble CD80 biologic for cancer treatment, along with related assets. BriaPro will hold worldwide development and commercialization rights and pay UMBC a 2% royalty on future product sales, while BriaCell will provide up to $3 million in research funding via a credit facility and receive nearly 24 million BriaPro shares, raising its ownership to about 78% pending minority shareholder approval and an independent valuation, reflecting a strategic bid to accelerate development of the potentially transformational anti-cancer agent and integrate it with BriaCell’s broader immunotherapy portfolio.

data.title: JFB Construction Raises Capital Amid AI Robotics Merger
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

JFB Construction Holdings Class A ( (JFB) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On February 13, 2026, JFB Construction Holdings signed a merger agreement with Xtend Reality Expansion and newly formed Xtend AI Robotics, Inc., under which an Israeli shell subsidiary will merge into Xtend and a second subsidiary will then merge into JFB, leaving both Xtend and JFB as wholly owned units of Newco. The deal structure will give Xtend shareholders at least 70.5% of Newco and JFB stockholders about 19.9%, with detailed provisions to roll over JFB and Xtend equity into Newco stock and options, plus up to 20 million additional Newco shares as earnout for Xtend investors tied to 2026 and 2027 results.

The transactions are targeted to close in mid‑2026, subject to extensive regulatory clearances, stockholder approvals and a $110 million minimum cash condition that is expected to be met largely via warrant exercises, while a series of support agreements lock in key shareholders on both sides and restrict post‑closing share transfers for 180 days. Alongside the merger signing, JFB completed a private placement on February 18, 2026, raising about $9.2 million in net proceeds from institutional investors, bolstering liquidity for the Xtend SAFE investment and underscoring investor backing for the shift toward an AI‑robotics‑focused public platform.

More about JFB Construction Holdings Class A

JFB Construction Holdings Class A operates as a publicly traded holding company that is now positioning itself as the corporate vehicle for a proposed combination with Xtend Reality Expansion Ltd., an AI-driven robotics and extended‑reality technology firm. The merged structure will be organized under a new Delaware corporation, Xtend AI Robotics, Inc. (“Newco”), with Newco seeking a Nasdaq listing under the ticker “XTND.”

Average Trading Volume: 67,950

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $182.7M

For an in-depth examination of JFB stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.title: SEALSQ Posts 66% Revenue Jump for 2025 but Projects Wider Loss on Heavy R&D Spend
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SEALSQ Corp ( (LAES) ) has provided an update.

On February 17, 2026, SEALSQ Corp reported preliminary unaudited results for the year ended December 31, 2025, highlighting revenue of $18 million, up 66% year-on-year, with fourth-quarter revenue doubling to $8 million from $4 million a year earlier. Growth was driven by renewed demand in core semiconductor and PKI lines, about $3.5 million in contributions from acquired design house IC’ALPS SAS since August 4, 2025, and early traction in post-quantum technology programs.

Despite the strong top-line expansion, SEALSQ projected a wider 2025 net loss of $30 million to $40 million versus a $21 million loss in 2024, citing heavier R&D and sales spending to launch a post-quantum semiconductor range, higher operating costs from IC’ALPS consolidation, and around $9 million of non-cash share-based compensation. The company ended 2025 with more than $425 million in cash and deployed roughly $30 million into strategic stakes across custom chip design, edge security, secure IoT satellite links, KYC platforms, and quantum chips, underscoring an aggressive investment-led growth and integration strategy whose final financial impact will only be clear once audited results are released by March 31, 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (LAES) stock is a Hold with a $5.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SEALSQ Corp stock, see the LAES Stock Forecast page.

Its activities span custom semiconductor design, edge security, secure satellite communications for IoT, financial-sector KYC infrastructure, and quantum chip design, reflecting a strategy of vertical integration across secure hardware and cryptography markets.

data.title: Renegade Gold Unveils 809,000-Ounce Resource at Rebel Deposit in Red Lake
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Renegade Gold ( (TSE:RAGE) ).

Renegade Gold has reported a new mineral resource estimate for its Rebel Gold Deposit in Ontario’s Red Lake district, outlining 370,000 ounces of gold in the Indicated category and 439,000 ounces in the Inferred category. The resource supports both open pit and underground mining scenarios, with open pit material averaging 2.07 g/t Au Indicated and 2.42 g/t Au Inferred, and an underground Inferred component grading 4.91 g/t Au.

Recent drilling has extended mineralization beyond 700 metres depth, more than doubling prior drill coverage and highlighting substantial expansion potential between 300 metres and 700 metres as well as at depth. Renegade is planning a 2026 drilling program aimed at growing the resource toward the one-million-ounce level and positioning Rebel as a meaningful contributor to existing milling capacity in the Red Lake camp.

More about Renegade Gold

Renegade Gold Inc. is a Canadian mineral exploration company focused on gold assets in the Red Lake Mining District of Ontario. The company’s primary project, now called the Rebel Gold Deposit and formerly known as the Newman Todd Deposit, is 100%-owned and is being evaluated for both open pit and underground development, targeting growth in a prolific gold-producing camp.

Average Trading Volume: 59,190

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: C$22.04M

Cue Biopharma ( (CUE) ) has issued an update.

Cue Biopharma, Inc. has scheduled its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders for April 13, 2026, and set February 28, 2026 as the key deadline for shareholders seeking to submit proposals for inclusion in the company’s proxy materials under SEC Rule 14a-8.
